title: Development Loop
expectation: Use a worker-hosted feature-branch worktree as the only mutable implementation surface for active development in this process; never edit from the shared repository checkout, a manager-hosted worktree, or a worker shared checkout. Start each iteration by loading the target blueprint or blueprints for the current focus, then perform one implementation iteration, then return to the blueprint-loading step and continue looping. When choosing the next loop outcome, select whether the next iteration should fix a bug, improve the code architecture, or add tests, and route each of those outcomes back to the blueprint-loading step.
idlePrompt: Resume the development loop immediately by loading the relevant blueprints, completing one implementation iteration, and then choosing whether the next loop should fix a bug, improve the code architecture, or add tests before returning to the blueprint-loading step. Do not pause or block the loop, and do not give only a status update.

steps.title: Load the target blueprint or blueprints for the current development focus
Complete one implementation iteration on the current focus
